name: Google Gemini 3.1 Pro

strengths.notes: CONFIRMED across 2 projects with actual output review. Delivers working code snippets (R, JS, CSS), not just opinions. R/Shiny review included bslib theme config, ggplot2 theme functions, CSS Grid patterns, client-side JS for tab switching.

Both reviews addressed educational audience (16-year-olds, Chromebooks). R review specifically addressed projector crispness (SVG), trackpad-friendly navigation (pill buttons), and 'educational feel'.

strengths.trait: goes beyond prompt with novel suggestions
strengths.notes: R review proposed features not asked for: plotly::ggplotly() hybrid for interactivity, thematic package for auto-theming, 'Show R Code' buttons, PDF export for lab reports, reactable over DT. These were practical and relevant, not noise.

Flagged that 30 server-side renders every 30 seconds would choke Railway. Recommended lazy rendering via req(input$nav), SVG over PNG, execOnResize=FALSE. Caught client-side tab switching latency (100-200ms server roundtrip) and proposed JS-based solution.

web_ui_input.staging_strategy: file — write to staging dir, user drags into upload zone
web_ui_input.notes: User confirmed file upload works but Gemini is choosy about which files it accepts. DeepSeek handles uploads better. Long context window.

output_characteristics.format: structured sections with code snippets and summary checklists
output_characteristics.actionability: very high — working code in every recommendation

pattern_stability: high — same role, same quality across HTML/CSS and R/Shiny
